rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ROOSEVELT WATTS TRUST DTD. 6/7/06 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: The motion for summary judgment is denied. The Court finds that the moving party did not meet the initial burden of production. See Regents of Univ. of Calif. v. Superior Court (1996) 41 Cal.App.4th 1040, 1044 (motion for summary judgment must be supported by evidence establishing the moving party's right to the relief sought). Moreover, the Court finds that there are triable issues of material fact as to whether Mr. Roosevelt had sufficient capacity to execute the trust; The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than April 26, 2016 at 4:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE AMENDMENT AND RESTATEMENT OF THE BESSIE GIN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Requests For Admissions; And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Peter H. Bresler, Esq.; (Proposed] Order Granting Motion To Compel And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The Trustee Sandra Chan Yee's ("Trustee") Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Admission is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Dexter Chan ("Dexter") is ordered to provide further responses to those RFAs to which he did not provide any substantive response: RFA Nos. 12, 21, 23, 26, 27, 33, 36-52 within 10 days of notice of entry of this order. The remainder of the motion is denied. The Trustee's request for sanctions is GRANTED in part. Dexter Chan and his counsel are ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $1,635 to the Trustee. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, April 26, 2016.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE AMENDMENT AND RESTATEMENT OF THE BESSIE GIN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Form Interrogatories; And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Peter H. Bresler, Esq.; (Proposed] Order Granting Motion To Compel And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: The Trustee Sandra Chan Yee's ("Trustee") Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Dexter is ordered to respond to FI 17.1(a)-(d) for those RFAs to which he did not provide any substantive response: RFA Nos. 12, 21, 23, 26, 27, 33, 36-52. Dexter is also ordered to particularize the documents responsive to: FI 17.1(d) for RFAs 1-52 (with the exception of RFAs 29 and 32, which contain unqualified admissions); FI 15.1(c) for all material allegations and affirmative defenses; and, FI 12.3(c) (see C.C.P. § 2030.230 ["specification shall be in sufficient detail to permit the propounding party to locate and to identify, as readily as the responding party can, the documents from which the answer may be ascertained"]). A reference to an undifferentiated block of 13,000 documents is not sufficient. All further responses must be provided within 10 days of notice of entry of this order. The remainder of the Trustee's motion is denied. The Trustee's request for sanctions is GRANTED in part. Dexter Chan and his counsel are ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $1,635 to the Trustee. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, April 26, 2016.
